text:
GENITOURINARY ONCOLOGY CLINIC
FOLLOW-UP VISIT NOTE

ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: Genitourinary Medical Oncology
VISIT TYPE: Established Patient Follow-Up

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Margaret Okonkwo is a 64-year-old woman with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) with sarcomatoid features presenting for her scheduled routine follow-up while on cabozantinib 40 mg daily. She has a new complaint of mild right hip discomfort over the past few weeks.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Okonkwo is a 64-year-old woman with a well-established history of Stage IV (cT3bN2M1) clear cell renal cell carcinoma of the right kidney, initially diagnosed approximately 18 months ago. The primary tumor measured 11.8 cm at the time of resection and demonstrated sarcomatoid differentiation in 22% of the tumor volume with WHO-ISUP grade 4 histology, extensive tumor necrosis, lymphovascular invasion, and renal vein involvement with infrarenal IVC thrombus (Level I-II). At the time of diagnosis, she had bilateral pulmonary metastases and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. NGS profiling identified VHL p.R161* and PBRM1 splice-site loss-of-function mutations, along with SETD2 frameshift mutation and CDKN2A homozygous deletion — a genomic profile conferring particularly aggressive biology in the context of sarcomatoid differentiation. PD-L1 CPS was estimated at 55 on the biopsy specimen.

She underwent cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy with RPLND and IVC thrombectomy approximately 15 months ago, with final pathology confirming pT3b disease and 9/14 lymph nodes positive, the largest involved node measuring 3.1 cm with extranodal extension. First-line systemic therapy was initiated with ipilimumab (ipi) 1 mg/kg every 3 weeks x 4 doses plus nivolumab (nivo) 3 mg/kg every 3 weeks for induction, followed by nivolumab 480 mg every 4 weeks maintenance, per CheckMate 214 protocol. Her course on ipi/nivo was complicated by Grade 2 immune-related colitis (managed with oral budesonide and temporary ipilimumab hold) and Grade 2 hypothyroidism (managed with levothyroxine, continued indefinitely). She achieved an initial partial response at the first restaging (approximately -42% reduction in target lesion sum), but subsequently developed mixed response at 6 months with growth of the right lower lobe pulmonary lesion and a new right adrenal metastasis — the latter confirmed as metastatic ccRCC by CT-guided biopsy. She received SBRT 54 Gy in 3 fractions to the right lower lobe lesion. Restaging at 9 months on immunotherapy demonstrated systemic progressive disease with growth of multiple pulmonary lesions, a new hepatic metastasis in segment VI, and persistent adrenal disease. Nivolumab was discontinued and she was transitioned to second-line cabozantinib.

Cabozantinib was started at 60 mg orally daily, dose-reduced to 40 mg within the first month due to Grade 2 palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia (hand-foot syndrome, HFS) and Grade 2 diarrhea. She also developed Grade 2 hypertension during the first 6 weeks on cabo, managed with addition of lisinopril 10 mg daily (amlodipine had already been maximized). Restaging CT at 3 months on cabozantinib demonstrated stable disease with minor decreases in all measured lesions. Repeat restaging at 6 months confirmed continued stable disease, with all target lesions stable.

Today she presents at the 9-month mark on cabozantinib. She reports that overall she has been doing reasonably well and is comfortable with the current treatment. She notes a new right hip pain over the past three to four weeks — described as a dull, achy discomfort in the right posterior and lateral hip area, present at rest and mildly worsened with ambulation. She denies radiation of pain down the leg, weakness in the lower extremities, bowel or bladder dysfunction, or saddle anesthesia. She attributes it to "sleeping on a hard mattress" but reports it has not improved. Given the context of metastatic RCC with known osseous metastasis being a common site of spread — particularly relevant with her aggressive tumor biology, CDKN2A deletion, and sarcomatoid differentiation — a bone scan was ordered at her request and reviewed today.

Regarding her ongoing toxicities from cabozantinib, she continues to experience Grade 1 HFS predominantly on the right heel. She uses silicone heel cups, soft-soled footwear, and urea cream 40% applied twice daily, which has kept this manageable. She has 1-2 loose stools above her baseline per day (Grade 1 diarrhea), which she manages with dietary modification (low-residue diet, reduced fat intake) and occasional loperamide 2 mg as needed. She denies any blood in the stool. Fatigue remains Grade 1 — she is able to take light daily walks in the neighborhood and helps care for her grandchildren a few afternoons per week. She denies new rash, oral mucositis, significant nausea, dysphagia, or abdominal pain beyond the baseline mild cramping with loose stools.

She is taking levothyroxine 100 mcg daily for her checkpoint inhibitor-associated hypothyroidism, which has been well-controlled on this dose (TSH 2.8 mIU/L at last check, 3 months ago). No new symptoms of hypothyroidism — no new cold intolerance, hair loss, or edema.

Blood pressure has been at goal on amlodipine 10 mg plus lisinopril 10 mg daily. No headaches, no visual disturbances.

She is adherent to cabozantinib and takes it daily on an empty stomach as instructed. No missed doses of significance in recent weeks.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: Grade 1 fatigue (stable, improved from nadir at time of progression). No fevers, chills, or unintentional weight loss. Appetite mildly diminished compared to pre-illness baseline but stable over the past several months; she estimates her current weight is stable.
Neurologic: No headaches, no focal weakness, no paresthesias in upper extremities. New right hip discomfort as detailed above. No saddle anesthesia, no bowel or bladder incontinence.
Musculoskeletal: Right hip/lateral hip pain x 3-4 weeks, dull and achy, no radiation to the knee or calf, mild impact on gait. No other joint pains.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, no palpitations, no lower extremity edema.
Pulmonary: No new dyspnea, no cough, no hemoptysis. Mild exertional dyspnea on walking uphill, stable and attributed to known bilateral pulmonary metastases.
Gastrointestinal: 1-2 loose stools above baseline per day, no blood, no severe cramping, no nausea at present.
Dermatologic: Grade 1 HFS, right heel most affected, manageable with current regimen.
Endocrine: No symptoms of thyroid dysfunction on current levothyroxine dose. No polyuria, polydipsia.
Genitourinary: Urinary stream and frequency normal. No hematuria (she is status post right nephrectomy; left kidney functioning appropriately).

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vital Signs:
- Blood pressure: 136/84 mmHg (left arm, seated)
- Heart rate: 76 bpm, regular
- Respiratory rate: 16 breaths/min
- Temperature: 36.8°C
- SpO2: 97% on room air
- Weight: 71.2 kg (stable; -0.5 kg from prior visit)
- ECOG Performance Status: 1

General: Alert, oriented, and in no acute distress. Pleasant woman, well-groomed, in no respiratory distress at rest. Conversational, non-cachectic.

Head and Neck: Normocephalic, atraumatic. No lymphadenopathy in cervical, supraclavicular, or submandibular chains. No thyromegaly. Oropharynx clear; no mucositis.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No jugular venous distension. Peripheral pulses 2+ bilaterally in upper extremities. No lower extremity edema.

Pulmonary: Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles, wheezes, or dullness to percussion. Effort and air movement adequate.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Well-healed midline laparotomy scar. Right flank surgical scar without erythema or tenderness. No palpable hepatosplenomegaly (liver edge not appreciated below costal margin). No palpable masses. Bowel sounds present and normal throughout.

Musculoskeletal/Neurologic: Mild tenderness to palpation over the right greater trochanteric region and the posterior right hip. No tenderness over the lumbar spine or sacroiliac joint. No pain with passive range of motion of the right hip through flexion, abduction, and internal/external rotation (full range maintained). Straight leg raise negative bilaterally. Normal lower extremity strength (5/5 bilaterally in hip flexors, quadriceps, hamstrings, tibialis anterior, and gastrocnemius). Sensation intact to light touch in both lower extremities including L4-S1 dermatomal distributions. Deep tendon reflexes 2+ bilaterally at knee and ankle. Gait slightly antalgic on right with mild hip guarding, but stable and independent.

Skin: Bilateral heels with Grade 1 palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia; right heel with focal hyperkeratosis approximately 1.5 cm area, no blistering, no fissuring. No erythema or swelling of palms. Skin overlying the right hip and thigh without erythema, ecchymosis, or warmth.

Lymph nodes: No peripheral lymphadenopathy in axillary or inguinal chains. No supraclavicular nodes appreciated.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS

Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 5.8 x 10^3/mcL (within normal limits)
- Hemoglobin: 10.2 g/dL (mild anemia, stable from prior — attributed to chronic disease/prior treatment; has been stable throughout cabozantinib course)
- MCV: 87 fL (normocytic)
- Platelets: 204,000/mcL (normal)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 138 mEq/L, Potassium: 4.2 mEq/L, Chloride: 102 mEq/L, Bicarbonate: 24 mEq/L
- BUN: 22 mg/dL, Creatinine: 1.2 mg/dL (stable; eGFR ~54 mL/min/1.73m2, appropriate for solitary left kidney status post right nephrectomy)
- Glucose: 98 mg/dL
- Total protein: 7.1 g/dL, Albumin: 3.8 g/dL (low-normal; no significant hypoalbuminemia)
- ALT: 38 U/L (normal), AST: 40 U/L (normal; improved from nadir at time of hepatic metastasis development)
- Alkaline phosphatase: 108 U/L (mildly elevated; previously 124 U/L at last restaging-era labs, trending slightly down)
- Total bilirubin: 0.7 mg/dL
- Calcium: 9.4 mg/dL (normal)
- Magnesium: 1.8 mEq/L (low-normal; to monitor given cabozantinib)

Thyroid Function (current):
- TSH: 2.5 mIU/L (therapeutic; within normal range on levothyroxine 100 mcg daily)
- Free T4: 1.1 ng/dL (normal)

Urinalysis: 1+ proteinuria (stable, consistent with prior assessment; 24-hour urine protein was 680 mg/24h when last formally checked, below threshold for dose modification; will recheck today's order as outpatient)

AM Cortisol (checked last visit per routine monitoring for immune-related adrenal insufficiency given prolonged prior nivolumab exposure): 13.8 mcg/dL (adequate; no evidence of adrenal insufficiency)

Lipid panel (checked today given statin therapy for hyperlipidemia and potential cabo effect on lipid metabolism): LDL 98 mg/dL, HDL 48 mg/dL, TG 142 mg/dL — within acceptable range.

---

IMAGING RESULTS

Bone Scan (whole body radionuclide bone scintigraphy with SPECT/CT, reviewed today):
Performed given new right hip pain. Result: NEGATIVE FOR OSSEOUS METASTATIC DISEASE. The scan demonstrates mildly increased tracer uptake in the right hip consistent with degenerative joint changes — specifically at the right acetabulum and femoral head interface, consistent with osteoarthritis. No focal areas of abnormal uptake suspicious for metastatic involvement in the pelvis, femora, lumbar spine, thoracic spine, or ribs. The left hip and contralateral skeleton are unremarkable.

Prior CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis (most recent, 3 months ago, per prior note — reviewed for reference):
Stable disease maintained at the 6-month cabozantinib timepoint. Right upper lobe 1.4 cm, left lower lobe 1.1 cm, left upper lobe 0.7 cm pulmonary lesions; previously irradiated right lower lobe lesion 1.3 cm; hepatic segment VI 1.2 cm; right adrenal 1.2 cm. No osseous lesions were seen on that CT, consistent with the bone scan today.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Margaret Okonkwo is a 64-year-old woman with Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation (22%), now approximately 9 months into second-line therapy with cabozantinib 40 mg daily, following progression on first-line ipilimumab/nivolumab at 9 months. She continues to maintain stable disease on cabozantinib, with the most recent formal restaging at 6 months demonstrating all target lesions stable. Overall disease trajectory remains favorable relative to her initial presentation given the aggressive biology.

1. METASTATIC ccRCC — STABLE DISEASE ON CABOZANTINIB
   - She is tolerating cabozantinib 40 mg daily with only Grade 1 residual toxicities at this time. She has been on this dose since the early-therapy reduction from 60 mg. Given ongoing stable disease and quality-of-life preservation, the current dose is appropriate and there is no indication for further dose modification at this time.
   - No clinical evidence of progression at today's visit: stable anemia, improved LFTs, stable creatinine. ALP has trended slightly down from prior elevated baseline, which is reassuring.
   - Scheduled CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast for restaging in approximately 3 months (9-month mark on cabo). No MRI brain planned given no neurologic symptoms, but a low threshold to image intracranially if any new symptoms develop given adrenal and hepatic metastatic burden.

2. RIGHT HIP PAIN — REASSURING BONE SCAN
   - Bone scan today is negative for osseous metastatic involvement. The pattern of mildly increased uptake at the right hip joint is consistent with osteoarthritis, which is a common degenerative finding in a 64-year-old patient with her activity level and body habitus.
   - Given the bone scan findings, this right hip pain is attributed to osteoarthritis rather than metastatic disease. She should be counseled on conservative management for osteoarthritis: acetaminophen 500-1000 mg TID as needed (safe in context of normal LFTs; preferred over NSAIDs given single kidney and proteinuria), activity modification, and physical therapy referral for targeted hip strengthening and gait optimization. Avoid NSAIDs given solitary kidney, proteinuria, and concurrent lisinopril (risk of acute kidney injury).
   - Orthopaedics or physical medicine consultation is not required at this time, but will refer if symptoms persist or worsen beyond 4-6 weeks despite conservative measures.

3. CABOZANTINIB TOXICITY MANAGEMENT
   a. Hand-Foot Syndrome (HFS), Grade 1:
      - Continue urea cream 40% to bilateral heels and palms twice daily.
      - Continue silicone heel cups and padded, soft-soled footwear.
      - Educated patient to report any worsening to Grade 2 features (blistering, pain interfering with activities) promptly, as this would prompt re-evaluation of dose or additional wound care.
      - No dose modification needed at this time.
   b. Diarrhea, Grade 1:
      - Continue dietary modifications (low-fat, low-residue diet); PRN loperamide as needed.
      - Reminded patient to stay well hydrated. Monitor for worsening diarrhea.
   c. Hypertension:
      - Well-controlled at 136/84 mmHg today on amlodipine 10 mg and lisinopril 10 mg daily.
      - Continue current antihypertensive regimen. Periodic BP checks at each visit.
   d. Proteinuria:
      - Will repeat spot urine protein:creatinine ratio today; if elevated, will consider 24-hour urine protein. If >2 g/24h, would consider cabozantinib dose hold/reduction.
   e. Fatigue, Grade 1:
      - Continue to encourage light aerobic activity as tolerated. Fatigue is multifactorial (anemia of chronic disease, single kidney, prior treatment) and is manageable at current level. No specific intervention planned.

4. HYPOTHYROIDISM — STABLE ON LEVOTHYROXINE
   - TSH 2.5 mIU/L today — within therapeutic range on levothyroxine 100 mcg daily. No medication change.
   - Next TSH recheck in 3 months at next scheduled visit.

5. ANEMIA OF CHRONIC DISEASE
   - Hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL, stable and normocytic. No symptomatic anemia (no dyspnea at rest, no significant exertional limitation beyond Grade 1 fatigue).
   - No indication for erythropoiesis-stimulating agents in this oncology context; iron studies were checked previously and were unremarkable for iron deficiency. Continue to monitor CBC.

6. HYPERTENSION / CARDIOVASCULAR RISK
   - BP well-controlled. Continue amlodipine 10 mg and lisinopril 10 mg daily.
   - Statin therapy continued per hyperlipidemia history; lipid panel acceptable today. No change needed.

7. RENAL FUNCTION (SOLITARY KIDNEY)
   - Creatinine 1.2 mg/dL stable; eGFR ~54 mL/min/1.73m2. This is expected for a post-right nephrectomy state and has been stable. Lisinopril has nephroprotective benefit.
   - Avoid nephrotoxic medications. Re-emphasize avoidance of NSAIDs.
   - Urine protein monitoring as above.

8. PALLIATIVE CARE / GOALS OF CARE
   - Patient continues co-management with the integrated palliative care team. At prior visits, her expressed priorities have been quality of life, maintaining independence, and being present for family events (including her grandchildren's activities). She reaffirmed these today.
   - She is currently achieving these goals: participates in daily walks, active with grandchildren, feels functional and engaged in daily life. Current therapy is consistent with her stated goals.
   - No changes to advance directive or code status discussion today; these remain on file and were previously reviewed.
   - Symptom burden remains at manageable Grade 1 levels across all domains.

RETURN VISIT: In 3 months, or sooner if new symptoms develop (worsening hip pain, new focal neurologic symptoms, new bone pain, change in bowel or bladder function, or significant toxicity escalation from cabozantinib).

RESTAGING IMAGING: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast to be scheduled in approximately 3 months.

FOLLOW-UP LABS: Repeat TSH, CBC, CMP, LFTs, and spot urine protein:creatinine ratio at next visit.

Total visit time: 40 minutes (more than 50% of encounter spent on counseling and coordination of care).

Electronically signed,
Genitourinary Medical Oncology Attending
